```python
# tests/test_reverse_words.py
import pytest

# If `reverse_words` is in another module replace the next line with:
# from your_module import reverse_words
from reverse_words import reverse_words


# ---------- Happy-path tests ----------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "original, expected",
    [
        ("hello world", "world hello"),
        ("The quick brown fox", "fox brown quick The"),
        ("one two three four five", "five four three two one"),
    ],
)
def test_reverse_words_basic(original, expected):
    assert reverse_words(original) == expected


# ---------- Edge-case tests ----------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "original, expected",
    [
        # Single word – nothing to reverse
        ("python", "python"),
        # Empty string
        ("", ""),
        # String that is only whitespace
        ("   \t  \n  ", ""),
        # Leading / trailing / repeated internal whitespace is squeezed to one space
        ("  hello   world  ", "world hello"),
        # Tabs and new lines are treated as whitespace by .split()
        ("hello\tworld\npytest", "pytest world hello"),
        # Unicode text
        ("こんにちは 世界", "世界 こんにちは"),
    ],
)
def test_reverse_words_edge_cases(original, expected):
    assert reverse_words(original) == expected


# ---------- Error-case tests ----------
@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [None, 123, 3.14, ["hello", "world"], {"text": "hi"}])
def test_reverse_words_invalid_input_raises_attribute_error(bad_input):
    # Objects that do not implement .split() should raise AttributeError
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        reverse_words(bad_input)
```
